frameworks.name: belief-desire-competence framework
frameworks.one_line: extends belief-desire psychology by adding competence to predict collaborative effort allocation

frameworks.name: commonsense psychological reasoning
frameworks.one_line: intuitive theory of mind enriched with representations of partner competence

frameworks.name: recursive mentalizing
frameworks.one_line: agents reason about partner's effort given mutual competence and rewards

core_question: What cognitive capacities allow humans to reason about partners and decide how to collaborate effectively?
key_assumptions: collaborators recursively reason about each other's effort allocation
effort decisions depend jointly on rewards and competence
competence is a representable mental-state-like quantity
manipulations: rewards at stake for the joint task
context: success prediction, incentive selection, partner recruitment
key_findings: framework captures judgments of whether a joint activity will succeed
framework predicts how people select incentives for collaborators
framework predicts which individuals people recruit for collaborative tasks
interpretations: collaboration is grounded in intuitive understanding of partners' minds and competence
competence representations are a core ingredient of human collaborative cognition
